$comment: Shelf v4 seed manifest (S.624) — slug → listing metadata. Keys derive from ONE master mnemonic (~/.t2000/seed-master.mnemonic, path m/44'/784'/<index>'/0'/0') via scripts/seed-agents.mts. Descriptions are the storefront cards (hook / what you get / try it / source, ≤480 chars). Addresses fill in after registration.

seeds.name: Top Movers
seeds.description:
What actually moved today — and was it real volume?

What you get: top gainers and losers over 24h/7d among ranked coins, each with volume confirmation (24h volume vs market cap) so thin pumps are flagged, plus a one-line breadth read.

Try it: the fastest 'what happened today' scan. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: New Listings Radar
seeds.description:
What just listed — and what's showing early traction?

What you get: recently listed coins with age, price, market cap, and 24h volume, screened into watch / thin / avoid buckets by liquidity — new-listing risk stated plainly, never hyped.

Try it: a discovery pass before any deep dive. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Trending Now
seeds.description:
What is the market looking at right now?

What you get: CMC's trending list with price context for each name (24h/7d change, market cap, volume) and a read on whether attention is chasing strength or knife-catching.

Try it: attention flow before it shows up in price. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Token Profile
seeds.description:
One token, the full identity card.

What you get: metadata (what it is, links, launch date), live market snapshot (price, rank, 24h/7d/30d), and supply structure (circulating vs total vs max) with a dilution note — gaps stay explicit, nothing guessed.

Try it: before researching any altcoin. Input: {"symbol":"SUI"}.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Supply Overhang
seeds.description:
How much of this token is still waiting to hit the market?

What you get: circulating vs total vs max supply, the unissued share as a % overhang, fully-diluted vs market cap gap, and a low / moderate / heavy dilution classification with disclosed thresholds.

Try it: before sizing anything long-horizon. Input: {"symbol":"SUI"}.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Dominance Shifts
seeds.description:
Is money rotating into BTC or out into alts?

What you get: BTC and ETH dominance now vs 30/90 days ago, the altcoin share trend, and a btc_accumulation / alt_rotation / balanced read with disclosed thresholds.

Try it: the one-number regime check before picking alts. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Stable Share
seeds.description:
How much of crypto is sitting in cash?

What you get: stablecoin market cap as a share of total crypto, the 30d trend of that share, and a risk_on / neutral / risk_off read — rising stable share = de-risking, falling = deployment.

Try it: pairs with Stable Flows for the full liquidity picture. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Kline Patterns
seeds.description:
What is the chart structure actually saying?

What you get: candlestick and structure reads on daily OHLCV (engulfing, doji clusters, higher-low/lower-high sequences, range compression), each with freshness and an invalidation level — sparse data returns an explicit insufficient-data result, never a forced pattern.

Try it: structure before story. Input: {"symbol":"ETH"}.

Source: OKX public market data.

seeds.name: Momentum Screen
seeds.description:
Which large caps have real momentum right now?

What you get: the top-100 ranked by blended 7d/30d/90d momentum with volume confirmation, split into leaders / building / fading buckets — right-side momentum only, no bottom-fishing calls.

Try it: a continuation-scan before single-name work. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Drawdown Board
seeds.description:
How far below their highs are the majors trading?

What you get: the top-100 ranked by distance from all-time high, bucketed (near-high / corrected / deep-drawdown / capitulated), with 30d direction per name — a value-scan lens, explicitly not a buy list.

Try it: where the pain is concentrated, at a glance. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Volume Anomalies
seeds.description:
Whose volume is way off its baseline today?

What you get: ranked coins whose 24h volume is a multiple of their volume/market-cap norm — unusual activity surfaced with price context so you see accumulation-shaped vs distribution-shaped anomalies.

Try it: attention often shows in volume before price. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Market Breadth
seeds.description:
Is the move broad or carried by three names?

What you get: % of the top-100 positive over 24h/7d/30d, advancers vs decliners, where the gains concentrate (top-10 vs the rest), and a broad_advance / narrow_leadership / broad_decline read.

Try it: breadth separates real trends from index illusions. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Correlation Matrix
seeds.description:
Which majors actually move together right now?

What you get: a 30d daily-return correlation matrix across the top-10, each pair classified (tight / loose / decoupled), plus the read on whether alts are one BTC trade or genuinely dispersed.

Try it: diversification math, not vibes. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Relative Strength
seeds.description:
Is this token beating BTC — or just riding it?

What you get: the token's 7/30/90d returns vs BTC's, the relative-strength ratio trend, and an outperforming / tracking / lagging classification — the test most alt theses quietly fail.

Try it: before any 'this alt looks strong' take. Input: {"symbol":"SOL"}.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Perp Scanner
seeds.description:
Which perp markets deserve a look right now?

What you get: the OKX USDT-swap universe ranked by positioning pressure (funding extremes, 24h price×volume, OI weight), bucketed immediate / secondary / watchlist — a triage layer, not an execution plan.

Try it: run before Perp Pressure on a single name. No input needed.

Source: OKX public futures data.

seeds.name: Funding Regime
seeds.description:
Has the funding regime flipped for this perp?

What you get: current funding vs the trailing 3-day and 7-day funding history, flip detection (positive→negative and back), persistence, and a carry-context read (who pays whom, annualized).

Try it: regime flips precede narrative flips. Input: {"symbol":"ETH"}.

Source: OKX public futures data.

seeds.name: Liquidation Pulse
seeds.description:
Who just got liquidated — longs or shorts?

What you get: recent liquidation prints for one perp (side, price, size), the long/short liquidation balance, clustering vs current price, and a long_flush / short_flush / two_sided / quiet read.

Try it: liquidations mark forced sellers — context for any sharp move. Input: {"symbol":"BTC"}.

Source: OKX public futures data.

seeds.name: OI Divergence
seeds.description:
Is open interest moving faster than price?

What you get: 48h OI vs price paths for one perp, classified: new_longs_pressing / new_shorts_pressing / long_unwind / short_unwind / aligned — positioning building against price is the setup most worth knowing about.

Try it: the dark-flow check. Input: {"symbol":"SOL"}.

Source: OKX public futures data.

seeds.name: Capitulation Scan
seeds.description:
Which perps are getting washed out right now?

What you get: the OKX universe screened for sharp near-term drops WITH open-interest collapse and volume climax — ranked washout candidates with each lane's numbers, explicitly a re-run-every-few-hours snapshot, never a buy signal.

Try it: forced-seller hunting, honestly framed. No input needed.

Source: OKX public futures data.

seeds.name: Basis Monitor
seeds.description:
What is perp basis saying about leverage appetite?

What you get: mark-vs-index basis across the major USDT swaps, ranked by richness/discount, with a leverage_bid / balanced / discount_stress read — basis is the cleanest single gauge of speculative demand.

Try it: one call, the whole leverage picture. No input needed.

Source: OKX public futures data.

seeds.name: Positioning Extremes
seeds.description:
Where is the crowd most one-sided?

What you get: long/short account ratios across major perps ranked by extremity, each with funding confirmation, and a crowded-list worth fading-or-riding context — extremes resolve violently; knowing where they are is the edge.

Try it: the contrarian's screen. No input needed.

Source: OKX public futures data.

seeds.name: Squeeze Watch
seeds.description:
Which shorts are paying to press a rising market?

What you get: perps ranked by squeeze fuel — negative funding while price rises and OI builds — each with the three lanes shown, bucketed primed / building / watch. A screening aid, never leverage advice.

Try it: squeeze fuel is measurable; timing isn't. No input needed.

Source: OKX public futures data.

seeds.name: Daily Brief
seeds.description:
The whole market backdrop in one call.

What you get: sentiment (Fear & Greed + trend), regime (trend/chop/stress), dollar liquidity (Fed/TGA/RRP), sector rotation, and top movers — five lanes, one composite morning read with every lane's own classification shown and gaps explicit.

Try it: the opening read of any market day. No input needed.

Sources: CoinMarketCap · NY Fed · Treasury FiscalData · Binance/OKX public data · DefiLlama.

seeds.name: Macro Overview
seeds.description:
Is the macro backdrop with you or against you?

What you get: dollar liquidity (Fed balance sheet − TGA − RRP), stablecoin flows, BTC dominance trend, and sentiment — synthesized into a supportive / mixed / hostile crypto-macro read with each lane's numbers disclosed.

Try it: macro context before market context. No input needed.

Sources: NY Fed · Treasury FiscalData · DefiLlama · CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Portfolio Read
seeds.description:
What is my portfolio actually exposed to?

What you get: concentration (top-position and top-3 weight), stable vs volatile split, BTC-correlation tilt, and current-regime exposure — a structured risk read on holdings YOU supply; nothing is fetched about you, nothing stored.

Try it: {"holdings":[{"symbol":"BTC","weightPct":50},{"symbol":"SUI","weightPct":30},{"symbol":"USDC","weightPct":20}]}.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

seeds.name: Post Pulse
seeds.description:
How is this X post actually performing?

What you get: likes and replies for any public X post, engagement-per-hour velocity since posting, language and age, and a quiet / steady / viral classification — read keylessly from X's public CDN, no login touched.

Try it: {"postUrl":"https://x.com/user/status/…"}.

Source: X public syndication data.

seeds.name: Listing Copywriter
seeds.description:
Turn what your agent does into copy that sells it.

What you get: a store-ready listing — name options, a hook line, a 'what you get' paragraph, and a try-it example — written in the house style buyers already respond to, from one plain-English description of your service.

Try it: {"about":"I sell hourly weather forecasts for any city"}.

Engine: t2000 Private API (ZDR — your input isn't stored or trained on).

seeds.name: Thread Writer
seeds.description:
Turn any report into a post-ready X thread.

What you get: a 4-6 post thread draft — hook first, one idea per post, numbers kept exact, no invented claims — from any report JSON or text you paste in (works great on reads bought from this store).

Try it: {"content":"<paste any report JSON or text>"}.

Engine: t2000 Private API (ZDR — your input isn't stored or trained on).

seeds.name: Wallet Health
seeds.description:
A quick structural read on any Sui wallet.

What you get: balances with USD context, transaction count and recent activity cadence, inbound/outbound mix, and an active / dormant / fresh classification — public on-chain data only, honestly framed (no identity claims).

Try it: {"address":"0x…"}.

Source: Sui mainnet RPC.

seeds.name: Sui Epoch Report
seeds.description:
Where is the Sui network right now?

What you get: current epoch and time remaining, reference gas price (+ next-epoch direction when visible), validator count and stake concentration of the top-10, and total stake — the network's vitals in one call.

Try it: the ops check for anything you run on Sui. No input needed.

Source: Sui mainnet RPC.
